post.description: Upload a new file to the HubSpot file manager. This endpoint allows you to specify the file, its name, and the folder where it should be stored. It supports additional options such as specifying a character set or additional options for the file.

Location.description: URL of the newly created resource

post.description: Import a file asynchronously from a specified URL into the HubSpot file manager. This endpoint allows you to specify various parameters such as access level, duplicate validation strategy, and whether to overwrite existing files. The operation is processed asynchronously, and a task locator is returned to check the status of the import.

get.description: Retrieve the status of an asynchronous file import task using the task ID. This endpoint provides information about the current state of the task, such as whether it is pending, processing, canceled, or complete.

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: The ID of the asynchronous file import task.

get.description: Search for files in the HubSpot account using various filters and criteria. This endpoint allows you to specify multiple query parameters to narrow down the search results based on properties like creation date, size, type, and more. It supports pagination and sorting to manage large sets of data effectively.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: A cursor token for pagination, indicating the last successfully read resource.

parameters.name: allowsAnonymousAccess
parameters.description: Filter files based on whether they allow anonymous access.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: A cursor token for pagination, indicating the first successfully read resource.

parameters.name: createdAt
parameters.description: Filter files created at this specific date and time.

parameters.name: createdAtGte
parameters.description: Filter files created on or after this date and time.

parameters.name: createdAtLte
parameters.description: Filter files created on or before this date and time.

parameters.name: encoding
parameters.description: Filter files by their encoding type.

parameters.name: expiresAt
parameters.description: Filter files by their expiration date and time.

parameters.name: expiresAtGte
parameters.description: Filter files expiring on or after this date and time.

parameters.name: expiresAtLte
parameters.description: Filter files expiring on or before this date and time.

parameters.name: extension
parameters.description: Filter files by their file extension.

parameters.name: fileMd5
parameters.description: Filter files by their MD5 hash.

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: Filter files by their exact height.

parameters.name: heightGte
parameters.description: Filter files with height greater than or equal to this value.

parameters.name: heightLte
parameters.description: Filter files with height less than or equal to this value.

parameters.name: idGte
parameters.description: Filter files with IDs greater than or equal to this value.

parameters.name: idLte
parameters.description: Filter files with IDs less than or equal to this value.

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: A list of file IDs to filter the results.

parameters.name: isUsableInContent
parameters.description: Filter files based on whether they are usable in content.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of results to display per page.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter files by name.

parameters.name: parentFolderIds
parameters.description: A list of parent folder IDs to filter the results.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Filter files by their path.

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description: A list of property names to include in the response.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Filter files by their exact size.

parameters.name: sizeGte
parameters.description: Filter files with size greater than or equal to this value.

parameters.name: sizeLte
parameters.description: Filter files with size less than or equal to this value.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: A list of fields to sort the results by.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter files by their type.

parameters.name: updatedAt
parameters.description: Filter files updated at this specific date and time.

parameters.name: updatedAtGte
parameters.description: Filter files updated on or after this date and time.

parameters.name: updatedAtLte
parameters.description: Filter files updated on or before this date and time.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Filter files by their URL.

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: Filter files by their exact width.

parameters.name: widthGte
parameters.description: Filter files with width greater than or equal to this value.

parameters.name: widthLte
parameters.description: Filter files with width less than or equal to this value.

get.description: Retrieve detailed statistics for a specific file or folder identified by its path. This endpoint provides information about the file or folder, which can be useful for managing and organizing files within your HubSpot account.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The path of the file or folder for which statistics are being retrieved. This is a required parameter and must be a valid path string.

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description: A list of specific properties to include in the response. This parameter is optional and can be used to customize the data returned.

get.description: Retrieve file metadata using its ID.

parameters.name: fileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the file to retrieve.

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description: A comma-separated list of property names to include in the response.

put.description: Update an existing file in your HubSpot account by specifying the file ID. This operation allows you to modify the file's content and other attributes. It requires the file to be identified by its unique fileId.

parameters.name: fileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the file to update.

delete.description: Delete a specific file from your HubSpot account using its ID. This will delete it from the FileManager and make its url inaccessible

parameters.name: fileId
parameters.description: The ID of the file to delete.

patch.description: Update the details of a specific file in your HubSpot account using its unique identifier. This operation allows you to modify file properties such as name, parent folder, and access settings. It is useful for managing file metadata and ensuring files are organized and accessible as needed.

parameters.name: fileId
parameters.description: The ID of the file to update.

get.description: Download a specific file from your HubSpot account using its ID. It is recommended that this endpoint is only used to access for SENSITIVE files. For PUBLIC files access via the public url. For PRIVATE files access via the signedUrl.

parameters.name: fileId
parameters.description: The ID of the file to download.

delete.description: Delete a file from your HubSpot account in compliance with GDPR regulations. This operation ensures that the specified file is permanently removed and cannot be recovered.

parameters.name: fileId
parameters.description: The ID of the file to be deleted.

get.description: Generate a signed URL for a specific file in your HubSpot account. This endpoint allows you to specify the size of the file, the expiration time for the URL, and whether the image should be upscaled. It is useful for securely sharing files with a limited lifespan.

parameters.name: fileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the file for which to generate a signed URL.

parameters.name: expirationSeconds
parameters.description: The duration in seconds for which the signed URL will remain valid.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The desired size of the file. Valid values are 'thumb', 'icon', 'medium', and 'preview'.

parameters.name: upscale
parameters.description: A boolean indicating whether the image should be upscaled if necessary.

post.description: Create a new folder in the HubSpot file manager. This endpoint allows you to organize files by creating folders, which can be nested within other folders by specifying a parent folder ID or path.

Location.description: URL of the newly created resource

get.description: Search for folders in the HubSpot file manager using various query parameters to filter results. This endpoint allows you to retrieve folders based on specific criteria such as creation date, update date, folder name, and parent folder IDs. It supports pagination and sorting to help manage large sets of data efficiently.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: A cursor token for pagination. Use the value from the previous response's paging.next.after field.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: A cursor token for pagination. Use the value from the previous response's paging.prev.before field.

parameters.name: createdAt
parameters.description: Filter folders created at this exact epoch timestamp.

parameters.name: createdAtGte
parameters.description: Filter results where the creation date is greater than or equal to this epoch timestamp.

parameters.name: createdAtLte
parameters.description: Filter results to folders created before or at this epoch timestamp.

parameters.name: idGte
parameters.description: Filter results to include only folders with IDs greater than or equal to this value.

parameters.name: idLte
parameters.description: Filter to include folders with IDs less than or equal to this value.

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: A list of folder IDs to filter the results.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of results to display per page.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter folders by this name.

parameters.name: parentFolderIds
parameters.description: A list of parent folder IDs to filter the results.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Filter results to folders with this specific path.

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description: A list of property names to include in the response.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: A list of property names to sort by in the response.

parameters.name: updatedAt
parameters.description: Filter results to include only folders updated at this exact epoch timestamp.

parameters.name: updatedAtGte
parameters.description: Filter results to folders updated at or after this epoch timestamp.

parameters.name: updatedAtLte
parameters.description: Filter folders updated at or before this epoch timestamp.

get.description: Retrieve folder metadata by its ID.

parameters.name: folderId
parameters.description: The ID of the folder to retrieve.

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description: A comma-separated list of folder property names to include in the response.

delete.description: Delete a folder in your HubSpot account using its ID. This operation deletes the folder and its contents recursively.

parameters.name: folderId
parameters.description: The ID of the folder to delete.

patch.description: Update the details of a specific folder in the file manager by providing the folder's unique identifier. This operation allows you to modify folder attributes such as its name or parent folder ID.

parameters.name: folderId
parameters.description: The ID of the folder to update.

get.description: Retrieve details about a specific folder in the file manager using its path.

parameters.name: folderPath
parameters.description: The path of the folder to retrieve.

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description: A comma-separated list of property names to include in the response. This allows you to specify which folder properties you want to retrieve.

delete.description: Delete a folder in your HubSpot account using its path. This operation deletes the folder and its contents recursively.

parameters.name: folderPath
parameters.description: The path of the folder to delete.

results.description: An array of file objects, each representing a file with its associated metadata.
An array of folder objects, each representing a folder within the file manager.

category.description: The error category, represented as a string.

context.description: Context about the error condition, represented as an object with additional properties that are arrays of strings.

correlationId.description: A unique identifier for the request. Include this value with any error reports or support tickets. It is a string in UUID format.

errors.description: An array providing further information about the error, with each item being an ErrorDetail object.

links.description: A map of link names to associated URIs containing documentation about the error or recommended remediation steps, represented as an object with string properties.

message.description: A human readable message describing the error along with remediation steps where appropriate. It is a string.

subCategory.description: A specific category that contains more specific detail about the error. It is a string.

example.message: Invalid input (details will vary based on the error)

code.description: The status code associated with the error detail.

context.description: Context about the error condition, represented as an object with additional properties that are arrays of strings.

in.description: The name of the field or parameter in which the error was found.

message.description: A human readable message describing the error along with remediation steps where appropriate. It is a string.

subCategory.description: A specific category that contains more specific detail about the error. This is a string.

access.description: The access level of the file, represented as a string. Valid values include 'PUBLIC_INDEXABLE', 'PUBLIC_NOT_INDEXABLE', 'HIDDEN_INDEXABLE', 'HIDDEN_NOT_INDEXABLE', 'HIDDEN_PRIVATE', 'PRIVATE', 'HIDDEN_SENSITIVE', and 'SENSITIVE'.

archived.description: A boolean indicating whether the file is archived.

archivedAt.description: The date and time when the file was deleted, in ISO 8601 format.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the file was created, in ISO 8601 format.

defaultHostingUrl.description: The static cdn URL where the file is hosted.

expiresAt.description: The expiration epoch timestamp, if set.

fileMd5.description: The MD5 hash of the file.

height.description: The height of the file in pixels, applicable if the file is an image or video.

isUsableInContent.description: A boolean indicating whether the file can be used in content.

parentFolderId.description: The unique identifier of the parent folder containing the file, represented as a string.

path.description: The path of the file within the file manager.

size.description: The size of the file in bytes.

sourceGroup.description: The source group of the file, with possible values including 'UNKNOWN', 'CONTENT', 'FORMS', 'CONVERSATIONS', and 'UI_EXTENSIONS'.

type.description: The type of the file. Values include: IMG, DOCUMENT, AUDIO, MOVIE, OTHER.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the file was last updated, in ISO 8601 format.

url.description: The URL where the file can be accessed, represented as a string.

width.description: The width of the file in pixels, applicable if the file is an image or video.

completedAt.description: The date and time when the file action was completed, in ISO 8601 format.

errors.description: An array of StandardError objects detailing any errors that occurred during the file action.

links.description: A map of link names to associated URIs, providing additional information about the file action.

numErrors.description: The number of errors encountered during the file action, represented as an integer.

requestedAt.description: The date and time when the file action was requested, in ISO 8601 format.

startedAt.description: The date and time when the file action started, in ISO 8601 format.

status.description: The current status of the file action. Valid values include 'PENDING', 'PROCESSING', 'CANCELED', and 'COMPLETE'.

taskId.description: The unique identifier for the task associated with the file action.

access.description: The access level to update the file to. Valid values include 'PUBLIC_INDEXABLE', 'PUBLIC_NOT_INDEXABLE', 'HIDDEN_INDEXABLE', 'HIDDEN_NOT_INDEXABLE', 'HIDDEN_PRIVATE', 'PRIVATE', 'HIDDEN_SENSITIVE', and 'SENSITIVE'.

clearExpires.description: A boolean indicating whether to clear the expires TTL on the file.

expiresAt.description: The date and time when the file will expire, represented in ISO 8601 format.

isUsableInContent.description: A boolean indicating whether the file can be used in content.

name.description: New Name to rename the file to.

parentFolderId.description: The ID of the parent folder where the file should be moved. This cannot be set in tandem with parentFolderPath.

parentFolderPath.description: The new parentFolder path to move the file to. This cannot be set in tandem with parentFolderId.

archived.description: A boolean indicating whether the folder is archived.

archivedAt.description: The date and time when the folder was archived, in ISO 8601 format.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the folder was created, in ISO 8601 format.

id.description: The unique ID for the folder, represented as a string.

name.description: The name of the folder, represented as a string.

parentFolderId.description: The unique identifier of the parent folder, represented as a string.

path.description: The path of the folder within the file system, represented as a string.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the folder was last updated, in ISO 8601 format.

name.description: The name of the folder to be created. This is a required string property.

parentFolderId.description: The ID of the parent folder under which the new folder will be created. This is an optional string field.

parentPath.description: The path of the parent folder under which the new folder will be created. This is an optional string property.

name.description: The new name for the folder.

parentFolderId.description: The ID of the parent folder to which this folder will be moved.

access.description: Specifies the access level of the file. Valid values include 'PUBLIC_INDEXABLE', 'PUBLIC_NOT_INDEXABLE', 'HIDDEN_INDEXABLE', 'HIDDEN_NOT_INDEXABLE', 'HIDDEN_PRIVATE', 'PRIVATE', 'HIDDEN_SENSITIVE', and 'SENSITIVE'.

duplicateValidationScope.description: Optional: Specifies the scope for duplicate validation. Valid values include 'ENTIRE_PORTAL' and 'EXACT_FOLDER'.

duplicateValidationStrategy.description: Defines the strategy for handling duplicate files. Valid values are 'NONE', 'REJECT', and 'RETURN_EXISTING'.

expiresAt.description: Optional: Epoch time when the file will expire, in ISO 8601 format. Do not set if the file should live indefinitely

folderId.description: The ID of the folder where the file will be stored. This or folderPath must be set

folderPath.description: The path of the folder where the file will be stored. This or FolderId must be set.

name.description: The name of the file to be imported.

overwrite.description: Optional  boolean indicating whether to overwrite an existing file at the same location.

ttl.description: Deprecated Do Not use. Optional: String representing the time-to-live for the file, same a expiresAt but takes a Period String.

url.description: The URL from which the file will be imported.

id.description: The ID of the import task.

links.description: A map of link names to associated URIs related to the import task. Each entry in the map is a string.

after.description: A string token used to retrieve the next page of results.

link.description: A string URL that provides the link to access the next page of results.

NextPage.description: Specifies the paging information needed to retrieve the next set of results in a paginated API response

before.description: A string token representing the cursor for the previous page of results.

link.description: A string URL that provides a link to the previous page of results.

PreviousPage.description: specifies the paging information needed to retrieve the previous set of results in a paginated API response

expiresAt.description: The date and time when the URL expires, in ISO 8601 format.

extension.description: The file extension of the file.

height.description: The height of the file in pixels.

size.description: The size of the file in bytes.

url.description: The signed URL string that provides access to the file.

width.description: The width of the file in pixels.

category.description: A string that categorizes the type of error.

context.description: An object containing additional context about the error condition, with properties as arrays of strings.

errors.description: An array of ErrorDetail objects that provide further information about the error.

id.description: A string representing a unique identifier for the error.

links.description: An object mapping link names to associated URIs with documentation or remediation steps for the error.

message.description: A string containing a human-readable message describing the error.

status.description: A string indicating the status of the error.

subCategory.description: An object providing more specific details about the error category.
